diagrams.title: Signal Handler Coding Rules Flowchart
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing correct signal handler implementation pattern
elements: Handler: Set volatile sig_atomic_t flag (ONLY this!)
Main program: Check flag in loop
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the recommended pattern: minimal handler, main program does work

code.title: Correct Signal Handler Pattern
code.description: Example of safe signal handling with volatile sig_atomic_t flag
code.explanation: This demonstrates the recommended pattern: handler only sets a flag (async-signal-safe), actual cleanup happens in main program where any function can be safely used.

code.title: Self-Pipe Trick Implementation
code.description: Using self-pipe trick for reliable signal-to-select communication
code.explanation: The self-pipe trick converts async signal delivery into synchronous I/O event. The handler only does async-signal-safe write(), while main program uses select() to wait for the signal and can then perform any operations safely.
code.timeComplexity: O(n) - select() blocks until signal or timeout

code.title: Handling EINTR in System Calls
code.description: Robust system call wrapper that handles EINTR
code.explanation: Demonstrates two approaches for handling EINTR: (1) manually retry in a loop when errno==EINTR, or (2) use SA_RESTART flag to let kernel automatically restart interrupted system calls. The manual approach gives more control.
code.timeComplexity: O(n) worst case with multiple interrupts
